barks.text: Or the week a man wonders if he's spent eight years building something a boy could drop.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: The Drowsy Lantern took in a new lodger last week. Margie said he asks about treasure at breakfast. ...Oh no. I know exactly whose tail that is.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: He winked twice. Innocent men don't wink twice. ...Or they wink exactly twice. I need more data.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: Like it belonged to him.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: Right weasel, wrong crimes. The town got its ending tonight and it's the wrong one. Tomorrow that gets harder to say out loud, not easier.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: The plaque moved. Ten years ago something was ADDED behind it — and the whole district watched it happen and called it a ceremony. The morgue will have the front page.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: Three ghosts on one map — and one of them is a private Vale boat landing, straight under the old boathouse. A family place. The kind of place you'd go to feel safe.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: No. He gets to say this part out loud himself.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: Two witnesses, one minute. Ferris saw the front; Milo saw the top. Between them: one whole figure, crossing one whole square. That's journalism — imperfect eyes, assembled honestly.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: You believed me enough to check. That's better than just believing me.
barks.speaker: milo

barks.text: The doubt and the joy are the same sentence read twice. Warren's row gets the stamp. Go count your lighthouses.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: One tail in the photograph. One weasel at the inn. The figure at the monument has a room number, and tomorrow it gets a stakeout.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: An insider, with legitimate key access. But Poppy had the only council key, and her window is closed... The board practically glows with it: is it the ONLY key?
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: Grandpa's first rule of photographs: they always show the truth. Just almost never the truth you developed them for.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: The photograph again. Two days it was a picture of a thief. Now it's a picture of a witness. Same silver, new truth — fourth time this photo has changed under my thumb.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: The Drowsy Lantern hums like a banked fire. Margie runs this room the way Ida runs locks — everything oiled, everything remembered, nothing rushed.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: No music down here. Just the clock, the paper, and fifty years of the town agreeing with itself in print. Grandpa's desk is three steps away and I'm not ready to sit at it.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: The river doesn't gossip. First thing in this town that hasn't. Mist coming off the water, and the old boathouse sitting there like a held breath.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: Six holes, one weasel, zero capsules. The town exhaled too early tonight, and tomorrow's paper decides whether the exhale was kind.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: Tuck's lockup: one cot, one window, and more dignity than most guest rooms. 'District business, not police business' — the whole cozy contract, in stone.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: Ferris Mott is communing with a map. Interrupting felt legally unwise.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: Prudence has the loupe up. When the loupe is up, the Archive speaks to no one.
barks.speaker: gary
